description: Lecture 13 discusses Black Box Reductions in mechanism design, focusing on Bayes Nash implementations and their properties. The lecture outlines goals for simple, polynomial-time mechanisms that are Bayesian incentive compatible, emphasizing the importance of direct revelation mechanisms in achieving expected utility.
topic_summary: The lecture covers Bayes Nash equilibrium and its implications for mechanism design, particularly in relation to welfare maximization and incentive compatibility. It introduces a methodology involving black box reductions to achieve Bayesian incentive compatibility from existing algorithms.
